figures.caption: Figure 7: CPDEM results for the 1D Neo-Hookean hyperelastic bar with parametric Young’s modulus E. Representative moduli E ∈ {0.50, 1.50, 3.00} are shown from top to bottom. For each E, the reference displacement (left), the CPDEM prediction (middle), and the pointwise absolute error |upred − uref | (right) are plotted along the axial coordinate; the relative L2 error is reported in the title of the error panel.

Figure 1: CPDEM architecture. The two encoders gθp and gθc are added to generate better representations for the material parameters and the collocation points coordinate. We also customize the manifold network gθg .

Figure 6: CPDEM versus FEM for the 2D linear elastic cantilever under parametric (E, ν). Three rows correspond to (E, ν) = (100, 0.25), (223, 0.30), and (500, 0.35) (units consistent with the traction and geometry in Fig. 5). Columns: FEM reference displacement magnitude (left), CPDEM prediction (middle), and pointwise absolute error (right) on the beam domain.
Figure 11: The rubber contact and sliding example.

Figure 4: CPDEM parametric performance for the 1D linear elastic bar across the parameter range of E. Left: exact and predicted displacement u(x) for several moduli (increasing E reduces the maximum displacement). Middle: pointwise absolute error |upred − uref | on a logarithmic scale. Right: relative L2 error (%) versus E for many sampled moduli in the trained range.

Figure 9: CPDEM versus FEM for the 2D Neo-Hookean cantilever under parametric (E, ν). Three rows correspond to (E, ν) = (800, 0.25), (979, 0.30), and (1200, 0.35). Columns: FEM reference displacement magnitude (left), CPDEM prediction (middle), and pointwise absolute error (right) on the beam domain (geometry as in Fig. 5).

Figure 3: CPDEM results for the 1D linear elastic bar with parametric Young’s modulus E. Three representative moduli E ∈ {0.50, 1.50, 3.00} are shown (top to bottom). For each E, the analytical displacement (left), the CPDEM prediction (middle), and the pointwise absolute error |upred − uref | (right) are plotted along the axial coordinate; the relative L2 error is reported in the title of the error panel.

Figure 8: Training loss convergence for the 2D Neo-Hookean cantilever (CPDEM). Total loss (left), energy loss (centre), and boundary loss (right) versus epoch; vertical axes use logarithmic scales. The dashed vertical line marks the switch from Adam to L-BFGS (approximately epoch 96).
